theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Consumer Staples (+2.79%) and Health Care (+2.19%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.17%) and Communication Svcs (-0.46%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.79% versus +1.79% for defensives and +0.77% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained -0.20% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -1.47%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up -0.06%, outperforming large caps. The VIX rose 6.76% to 16.73, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.30% to $3,988.30, crude oil was little changed at $78.88, and bitcoin slipped 0.59% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
